title: Interstellar
logline: A former NASA pilot must leave his family and travel through a wormhole to find a new habitable planet for humanity as Earth becomes uninhabitable due to ecological collapse.
antagonistOrConflict: Time, entropy, and the dying Earth; Dr. Mann's betrayal; the limitations of human survival
themes: The relationship between parent and child
The tension between individual needs and species survival

actBreaks.description: Cooper launches into space, leaving Earth and his family behind to begin the interstellar mission.

validation.ruling: Scene #55 (p43, 27.4%) shows the rocket igniting and rising from the pad—the irreversible moment Cooper leaves Earth and his children behind. This is the last scene before the story shifts entirely to space, marking the fundamental transformation from Earth-bound farmer to interstellar pilot. The story cannot return to its pre-launch state; Cooper has crossed the threshold into the mission.

actBreaks.description: Cooper and Brand successfully dock with the damaged Endurance after Dr. Mann's betrayal, preparing for the final sacrifice at Gargantua.

validation.ruling: Scene #272 (p127, 80.9%) shows Cooper and the crew in the damaged Endurance with hissing steam and debris, locking down bulkheads after the explosion, as they slip toward Gargantua. This is the final scene before Cooper must make the ultimate sacrifice (jettisoning himself), marking the climactic turning point where the mission's survival hangs by a thread and forcing the story into its final act of resolution.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality:
This is a three-act epic that uses relativity and time dilation as both scientific concept and emotional engine. Act One (scenes 1-55) establishes Earth's dying state and Cooper's recruitment, ending with his devastating departure. Act Two (scenes 56-272) is the longest section—the deep space mission itself—where the crew visits Miller's planet (losing years to time dilation), then Dr. Mann's planet (where betrayal nearly destroys them). The act break at scene 272 marks their survival of Mann's sabotage and the decision to use Gargantua's gravity. Act Three (scenes 273-367) accelerates into the tesseract sequence, Cooper's communication across time to Murph, and the resolution where he's reunited with his aged daughter before departing to find Brand.

The structure is deceptively straightforward but emotionally complex: it's a linear journey outward that becomes a circular journey inward (through the tesseract back to Murph's bedroom). The script uses extreme time dilation to create parallel timelines—Cooper ages slowly while Murph ages rapidly—turning what could be a standard space adventure into a meditation on parental love across impossible distances.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is deliberately uneven, mirroring the time dilation concept. Act One moves quickly through 55 scenes establishing the mission. Act Two expands massively (scenes 56-272, over 200 scenes) to contain the bulk of space exploration, with the Miller's planet sequence (scenes 108-153) creating the first major time jump where they lose 23 years in what feels like hours. The Dr. Mann betrayal sequence (scenes 163-272) accelerates into intense action. Act Three compresses again, with the tesseract sequence (scenes 320-348) using rapid cross-cutting between different time periods in Murph's bedroom to create urgency and emotional catharsis. The final scenes (349-367) provide denouement across two locations: Cooper Station and Edmunds' planet.

incitingIncident.description: Romilly explains that gravitational anomalies led to the discovery of a wormhole near Saturn that appeared forty-eight years ago and leads to another galaxy, placed there by unknown beings they call 'They.'

validation.ruling: Scene #38 is merely the discovery of NASA, not the inciting incident. Scene #42 (p29-35, INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - LATER) is where Professor Brand reveals the Lazarus missions, the wormhole, and asks Cooper to pilot the mission to save humanity—this shatters Cooper's world, establishes the main conflict (save humanity vs. stay with family), introduces irreversible stakes, and forces Cooper into the central decision that drives the entire story.

midpoint.description: Cooper tells the crew they must choose between Dr. Mann's ice world and Edmunds' desert planet because they lack fuel to visit both; Brand argues for Edmunds' planet based on her field expertise and Gargantua's sterilizing radiation, but Cooper decides they will go to Dr. Mann's planet because he is still transmitting.

validation.ruling: Scene #153 (p84, 53.5%) is a clear midpoint where the crew must choose between Dr. Mann's planet and Edmunds' planet after Miller's world disaster cost them years. This is a point of no return—they cannot visit both—and Cooper shifts from reactive (following the mission plan) to proactive (making the critical choice). The debate and vote fundamentally changes their trajectory and raises personal stakes as Brand advocates for Edmunds while Cooper chooses Mann.

climax.description: Cooper races faster down the world lines, explaining to Tars that the beings have infinite time and space but no way to find what they need, but he can find Murph through love and tell her how to find the moment, then looks at the watch on the shelf and realizes he can tell her how to save the world.

validation.ruling: Scene #334 is Cooper realizing he can send a message via gravity, but this is setup, not climax. Scene #342 (p146, INT. TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS) is the actual climax where Cooper frantically manipulates the second hand of the watch to transmit the quantum data to adult Murph in Morse code—this is the peak confrontation with time itself, the ultimate obstacle, demonstrating his arc completion (love transcending dimensions), and directly resolving the main story question of whether humanity can be saved.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE STRATOSPHERE - DAY
scenes.summary: A craft burns through the fringes of space.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A young pilot fights his buffeting craft as radio warns him he's too tight and threatens to shut down power despite his protests.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE STRATOSPHERE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The black and red sky starts spinning horrifyingly.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: The controls rip themselves free as the pilot shouts.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM, FARMHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cooper wakes from a nightmare about the crash and reassures his daughter Murph there's no ghost in the house before she returns to bed.

scenes.heading: EXT. COOPER’S FARM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Donald sweeps dust out of the farmhouse door onto the porch.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - MORNING
scenes.summary: At breakfast, Murph shows Cooper her broken lunar lander model and claims a ghost knocked it off her shelf along with books, while Tom mocks her and Cooper tells them to stop arguing.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper and Donald discuss Nelson torching his okra crop due to blight as the kids pile into the truck, and Donald suggests Cooper should pursue Miss Hanley.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DIRT ROAD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: While driving, Cooper teaches Murph to shift gears until a tire blows out, prompting Tom to invoke Murphy's Law and Murph to ask why she was named after something bad, which Cooper explains means making something work despite adversity.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK THROUGH FIELDS - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper spots an Indian surveillance drone and commandeers the truck to chase it through cornfields while working a laptop in Hindi, nearly hitting a harvester before the drone banks away.

scenes.heading: EXT. RIVERBANK - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper examines the downed drone and tells the kids it's been flying for ten years since Delhi mission control went down, then decides to salvage its components.

scenes.heading: EXT. COUNTY SCHOOL - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper drops Tom at school while Murph waits in the truck and draws a barcode in her notebook, warning Cooper about an upcoming meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. PRINCIPAL’S OFFICE - LATER (MORNING)
scenes.summary: The Principal tells Cooper that Tom will be a farmer rather than attend university due to low test scores, and Ms Hanley reveals Murph was suspended for bringing a corrected textbook to school that contradicted the approved version claiming the moon landings were faked.

scenes.heading: EXT. PICKUP TRUCK OUTSIDE SCHOOL - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper tells Murph he got her suspended, then receives a radio call from Boots reporting that the rebuilt combines have gone haywire.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper and Murph arrive at the farmhouse to find automated farm machines have gathered around it like animals, and Boots explains something is interfering with their compasses.

scenes.heading: INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper hears a bang from upstairs and heads up to investigate.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper finds books on the floor of Murph's bedroom and she shows him her notebook where she's been counting the spaces and trying to decode them as Morse code, but Cooper dismisses the ghost theory.

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT PORCH, FARMHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Donald and Cooper discuss the compass anomaly on the porch, and Donald reflects that Cooper was born in the wrong era, either too late or too early to belong in this dying world.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASEBALL FIELD - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At a Yankees game, Cooper tells Tom the school has agreed to let him work the farm, and Donald asks what a hot dog is as a dust storm approaches.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper speeds the truck through the advancing dust storm as the family stuffs rags into cracks and puts on surgical masks while black dust and lightning envelop them.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper wrestles Murph out of the truck as sheet metal smashes the windshield and Donald stumbles toward the house with Tom in near-zero visibility.

scenes.heading: INT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Inside the house, Cooper realizes dust is coming from upstairs and Murph runs toward the stairs despite his warning.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper shuts Murph's window and they both stare transfixed at dust falling unnaturally fast in lines and collecting in a pattern on the floor.
scenes.turningPointNote: The gravitational anomaly in Murph's room is revealed, setting in motion the discovery of coordinates that will lead Cooper to NASA.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAWN - MORNING
scenes.summary: Dawn breaks with the dust settled and calm restored.

scenes.heading: INT. TOM’S ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Murph slips out of Tom's bed and peeks into her bedroom.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper sits staring at the circular barcode pattern of dust, then tosses a coin across a line which shoots to the floor, confirming it's gravity.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME - LATER
scenes.summary: Donald tells Cooper to clean up the dust pattern while Cooper reaches for Murph's notebook and starts writing.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - LATER
scenes.summary: Murph fills a glass of water and picks up a plate of sandwiches.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper decodes the dust pattern as binary coordinates, translating thick and thin radial lines into number pairs.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper decodes the gravitational anomaly as coordinates, providing the location that will lead him to NASA's secret facility.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper and Murph pore over maps and locate the spot indicated by the coordinates.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper packs his truck for a trip while Murph protests being left behind, but he refuses to take her.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper calls for Murph to wait for Grandpa but receives no response.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON ROAD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper discovers Murph hiding under a blanket in the truck and she laughs as he nearly loses control, then he hands her the map to navigate.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLAINS APPROACHING MOUNTAINS - DUSK
scenes.summary: The pickup drives toward the darkening mountains.

scenes.heading: EXT. DIRT ROAD OFF MOUNTAIN PASS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cooper approaches a chain-link fence with bolt-cutters when spotlights blind him and an electric voice orders him to step away from the fence.

scenes.heading: INT. BRIGHT, INDUSTRIAL ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper wakes in a chair being interrogated by a machine about how he found the facility, and when he demands to know where Murph is and threatens the machine, it rises to full height and a woman's voice tells him to relax.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDERGROUND FACILITY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper follows Brand into a corridor with the machine Tars lurching behind, and when he asks to leave, Brand tells him it's not that simple because he knows about the place.

scenes.heading: INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper is reunited with Murph in a conference room where Professor Brand and others interrogate him about finding the facility, and he explains they learned the coordinates from a gravitational anomaly in Murph's bedroom that knocked books off shelves in a pattern.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper discovers NASA's secret facility and is reunited with Professor Brand, beginning his recruitment into the Lazarus mission.

scenes.heading: INT. UNDERGROUND FACILITY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Professor Brand shows Cooper the secret NASA facility and explains they were brought back after being shut down for refusing to bomb starving people.

scenes.heading: INT. GREENHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Professor Brand shows Cooper greenhouses and explains that blight has destroyed wheat and okra and will soon kill all corn, and that Earth's atmosphere is becoming 80 percent nitrogen with oxygen levels dropping.

scenes.heading: INT. VAST CIRCULAR CHAMBER (LAUNCH FACILITY) - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Professor Brand shows Cooper a rocket in a vast launch facility and explains they're not meant to save the world but to leave it, revealing the Lazarus missions sent twelve people through a wormhole near Saturn.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Lazarus mission and humanity's plan to leave Earth through the wormhole is revealed to Cooper.

scenes.heading: INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - LATER
scenes.summary: Romilly explains that gravitational anomalies led to the discovery of a wormhole near Saturn that appeared forty-eight years ago and leads to another galaxy, placed there by unknown beings they call 'They.'

scenes.heading: INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper realizes the entire facility is a space station and Professor Brand explains Plan A involves solving the gravity equation to launch it, though he hasn't solved it yet.

scenes.heading: INT. LABORATORY - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Brand shows Cooper Plan B, a population bomb containing five thousand fertilized eggs that could establish a colony through incubation and surrogacy if Plan A fails.

scenes.heading: INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE
scenes.summary: In his office covered with algebra, Professor Brand tells Cooper he's almost solved the equation and asks him to trust him, then questions what Cooper can tell his children about where he's going.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - LATE DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper's truck arrives at the farmhouse and Murph tears past Donald into the house.

scenes.heading: INT. HALL OUTSIDE MURPH’S ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper tries to open Murph's door but she has barricaded it and tells him to leave if he's going.

scenes.heading: EXT. PORCH - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Donald tells Cooper the world was never enough for him and warns not to trust the right thing done for the wrong reason, but Cooper insists the foundation is solid because next year won't come on Earth.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Cooper removes the barricade and tells Murph he has to fix things before leaving, but she refuses to talk, and he explains that parents become ghosts of their children's futures, promising to come back and asking her to solve the ghost mystery so he'll know when to return.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper's emotional farewell to Murph establishes the watch as their connection and sets up the promise that will drive the film's resolution.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper hugs Tom goodbye, tells him to look after the farm, and drives away without Murph coming out to say goodbye.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Murph grabs the watch and runs downstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. PICKUP TRUCK - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper drives away, checking the wheel-well where Murph hid before while a countdown begins over radio.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Murph races outside with the watch calling for her dad, but Cooper is already far down the road; she sobs as Donald comforts her.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DUSTY PLAIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper drives away with tears rolling down his cheeks as the countdown continues.

scenes.heading: INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: The rocket ignites and rises from the launch pad into the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. RANGER COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper experiences the rocket's force in his helmet as stages separate and he sees Earth's curve, breaking free of the planet's bonds.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper physically leaves Earth, crossing the point of no return on his mission and abandoning his children.

scenes.heading: EXT. UPPER ATMOSPHERE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The rocket tears upward into the sky.

scenes.heading: INT. RANGER COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper glances around the vibrating cockpit at his crew; Tars makes sarcastic jokes about his humor setting and blowing Cooper out the airlock.

scenes.heading: INT. RANGER COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper and Brand look down at Earth from orbit and discuss the difficulty of leaving; Cooper asks Tars about his honesty parameter and Tars moves to the rear airlock.

scenes.heading: EXT. EARTH ORBIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Rangers approach the ring module, fire retro-thrusters, and slide into the center to complete the U.S.S. Endurance.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Brand, Doyle, Romilly, and Tars float through the ring module powering up systems; Tars activates Case; Doyle tells Cooper he has control.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE (RANGER) - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper checks instruments and asks if they're ready to spin.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Doyle and Romilly strap in while Brand grabs a handhold.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand confirms they're set and Cooper hits a switch.

scenes.heading: EXT. EARTH ORBIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Thrusters fire on the Endurance and it begins rotating.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The crew settles in as gravity is established; Romilly struggles with the rotation and Brand suggests Dramamine.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - LATER
scenes.summary: The crew listens to Professor Brand over video link discussing their two-year trajectory to Saturn; Cooper asks him to watch his family, especially Murph.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper and Doyle check instruments while Professor Brand's voice continues over the radio.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand, Romilly, Tars, and Case strap in as Professor Brand recites Dylan Thomas.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper turns to Doyle and hits the thrusters.

scenes.heading: EXT. EARTH ORBIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance's main engines fire, pushing the craft out of Earth orbit and accelerating away from the planet.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Endurance leaves Earth orbit, beginning the interstellar journey with no possibility of quick return.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: The crew sets up cryo-beds; Cooper and Brand look at the diminishing Earth and discuss their three planetary prospects, including Wolf Edmunds whom Brand speaks of fondly.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTER SPACE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance slips away from Earth as Cooper's voice-over describes how amazing Earth looks from space.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORNFIELDS - DAY
scenes.summary: A line of dust slides across the horizon of the cornfields.

scenes.heading: EXT. FRONT PORCH, FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Donald and Murph see approaching vehicles; Professor Brand arrives in Cooper's truck with a message from Cooper, but Murph refuses to watch it and runs away.

scenes.heading: EXT. MARS - DAY
scenes.summary: The Endurance streaks away from Mars.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATURN - DAY
scenes.summary: The Endurance settles into orbit around Saturn.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper watches a compressed video message from Tom and Donald; Murph refuses to appear; Cooper puts in earbuds and leaves.

scenes.heading: INT. HAB POD, RING MODULE - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper finds Romilly staring out the window, anxious about the dangers of space; Cooper tries to comfort him with a metaphor about yachtsmen who couldn't swim.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance appears as a tiny speck before Saturn.

scenes.heading: INT. NAVIGATION, RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper examines images from the relay probe showing the other side of the wormhole; Brand announces they'll reach the wormhole in less than forty-five minutes and tells them to suit up.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper straps into the Ranger cockpit and tells the crew he's killing the spin as Romilly joins him, excited.

scenes.heading: EXT. SATURN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance stops rotating and heads toward a distorted blur of stars as it streaks past Saturn.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly points out the spherical wormhole and explains to Cooper why it's a sphere rather than a hole, demonstrating with folded paper.

scenes.heading: EXT. WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance swings around the wormhole with disorienting visual effects and fires retro-thrusters to descend toward it.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper asks about tricks for entering the wormhole; Doyle says no one knows and that only some of the previous explorers made it; Cooper pushes forward and lets gravity pull them toward the center.

scenes.heading: EXT. WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance crosses the threshold of the wormhole, passing into the distortion with no actual surface.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew enters the wormhole, leaving their galaxy entirely and crossing into unknown space with no guarantee of survival.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper and Doyle stare at the distortion of space ahead.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand and Romilly look out at reflections in a space beyond three dimensions.

scenes.heading: EXT. WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance moves through a tunnel of distorted reflections, seeming to accelerate but not getting closer to the far mouth.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper checks instruments but Doyle tells him they won't help inside the bulk; all they can do is record and observe.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand sees a shape bending spacetime inside the cabin; she reaches toward it and it distorts her hand without causing pain; she believes it's "them."

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper and Doyle watch the tunnel mouth streak toward them with growing stars and nebulae.

scenes.heading: EXT. FAR SIDE OF THE WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance slides out of the wormhole.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The instruments chirp as Doyle confirms they've arrived; Cooper and Doyle look out at the new galaxy.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew successfully emerges in the foreign galaxy, completing the wormhole transit and beginning the search for habitable worlds.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand's hand returns to normal and she flexes her fingers, calling the encounter "the first handshake."

scenes.heading: EXT. FAR SIDE OF THE WORMHOLE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The cosmos on the far side of the wormhole is more crowded with stars and nebulae.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - LATER
scenes.summary: Doyle reviews cached communications revealing Miller and Mann are still transmitting but Edmunds went dark three years ago; Romilly explains Miller's planet orbits dangerously close to the black hole Gargantua, where time dilation means one hour equals seven years on Earth.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew learns about the extreme time dilation at Miller's planet, establishing the devastating temporal stakes of their mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. BLACK HOLE, GARGANTUA - DAY
scenes.summary: The Endurance approaches Gargantua, a massive black hole visible by its distorting effect on starlight.

scenes.heading: INT. RANGER COCKPIT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper looks at Gargantua while Doyle and Brand point out Miller's planet; Cooper exchanges brief words with Case and gives the order to detach.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger detaches from the Endurance's ring module and fires retro-thrusters to begin its descent.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE BLACK HOLE, GARGANTUA - DAY
scenes.summary: The Ranger shoots down toward the black hole Gargantua.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper radios Romilly about the extreme gravitational forces they're experiencing during descent.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly studies the data and remarks that seeing the singularity inside Gargantua would solve gravity.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper asks if they can get information from the black hole, and Romilly explains nothing escapes the event horizon.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARGANTUA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger streaks over Gargantua's blackness toward Miller's planet, a dark-blue world.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper decides to use aerodynamics instead of thrusters to save fuel during atmospheric entry, and Case alerts Brand and Doyle to prepare.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger streaks down toward Miller's planet and begins encountering the stratosphere.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger shakes violently as Cooper refuses Case's suggestion to ease up and Doyle protests they're going too fast, but Cooper insists he needs to feel the air.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger glows white hot as it slices through flat clouds.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper gets a fix on the beacon and plans to spiral down while shaving speed, as Doyle looks nervously at Brand.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger bursts through clouds into clear air high above an endless ocean.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The crew observes the shallow water below as Cooper banks sharply and Case reports they're twelve hundred meters from the beacon.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger flies low, kicking up backwash from the shallow water.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper waits until they're close enough, then orders Case to fire the retro-rockets.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger's retro-rockets fire, killing its speed just above the surface, and the craft lands with gear holding it above the shallow water.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Everyone bounces from the impact, then Brand and Doyle hurry out while Case opens the hatch, letting in light and spray.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - MOMENTS LATER (DAY)
scenes.summary: Case, Brand, and Doyle exit the Ranger into knee-deep water and begin moving toward the beacon two hundred meters away, struggling with the planet's 130% Earth gravity.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper listens impatiently to their radio chatter from inside the cockpit.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Case stops at the beacon location and thrashes underwater while Brand and Doyle arrive confused about the signal's source.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper notices something in the distance—the mountains.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Case pulls damaged equipment from the seabed while Brand identifies it as Miller's beacon, then Brand spots floating objects toward the mountains.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper realizes the mountains are not mountains.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper radios that they're waves, and Brand sees the mountains are moving with white spray blowing from their tops.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew discovers Miller's planet is covered by massive tidal waves, fundamentally changing their understanding of the danger and Miller's fate.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper looks the other direction and sees a mountain wave bearing down on the ship.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper orders Brand back as she searches wreckage for the recorder, and Doyle sees the approaching wave and tells Case to get her.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper hits the dash in frustration and orders Brand to return immediately.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand insists they need Miller's data while Case runs toward her as she struggles with equipment in the water.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper orders Brand to go as she's pinned by wreckage, Case throws her onto his back and runs, Doyle stands mesmerized by the wave, and the scene cuts off mid-action.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper powers up as the wave fills his view, Case throws Brand inside and reaches for Doyle, but the Ranger tilts and rises, ripping Doyle from Case's hand as water rages across the hatch.
scenes.turningPointNote: Doyle is lost to the wave as the Ranger is forced to lift off, marking the mission's first casualty and the cost of the time dilation miscalculation.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger is sucked sideways up the face of the mountain wave.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand and Cooper are thrown across the cockpit as Case pulls Brand into her seat and the craft rolls repeatedly.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger reaches the top of the wave and rocks upright.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper drops into his seat as water pours off the canopy.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger tilts over the backside of the wave, surfs briefly, then pitches forward and tumbles down eight thousand feet.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The crew hangs on for dear life as they're thrashed mercilessly inside the tumbling craft.

scenes.heading: EXT. MILLER’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger comes to rest on the ground as the wave leaves it behind.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper jumps to the controls and powers up the electrics, but the engines won't respond.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger's gear lifts the craft as water floods out from underneath.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper and Brand argue bitterly about the mission and Doyle's loss while Case estimates forty-five to an hour for the craft to drain, and Brand reveals the time cost will be decades and explains Miller died soon after impact but her wreckage stayed together because time moved so slowly on the planet.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew realizes they've lost decades of Earth time due to the extreme time dilation near Gargantua, fundamentally altering the stakes of their mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger's rockets cough and steam as they attempt to restart.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper tries the engines again without success, then orders helmets on and has Case blow cabin oxygen through the thrusters to spark ignition as the wave approaches and the cockpit depressurizes.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger blasts clear of the mountain wave with a fiery explosion.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper and Brand enter the Endurance where Romilly reveals twenty-three years have passed, Brand shakes her head when asked about Doyle, and she tells Romilly there's nothing on Miller's planet for them while looking older and more fragile.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew confronts the devastating reality that 23 years have elapsed, Doyle is dead, Miller's planet is worthless, and everyone they knew on Earth has aged dramatically.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper enters the communications booth and begins watching messages spanning twenty-three years, starting with seventeen-year-old Tom introducing a new girlfriend and mentioning Murph crashed Grandpa's car.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper watches days of messages as Tom ages into his twenties and thirties, introduces his infant son Jesse, reveals Grandpa died and was buried next to Mom and Jesse, mentions he rarely sees Murph anymore, and concludes Cooper isn't listening and all the messages are drifting in the darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNICATION ROOM, NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph wipes tears from her face in the communication room as elderly Professor Brand in a wheelchair apologizes for intruding, and Murph wheels him out into the corridor.

scenes.heading: INT. CURVING CORRIDOR, NASA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Murph pushes Professor Brand through the corridor and tells him she just had something she wanted to get out, not that she's started talking to Cooper regularly.

scenes.heading: INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Professor Brand expresses fear that the crew will return to find Earth has failed, and Murph responds by suggesting they succeed instead, then they return to working on the equation with a finite set.

scenes.heading: INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - LATER
scenes.summary: Murph and Professor Brand eat sandwiches in the launch facility as he reflects that every rivet could have been a bullet and expresses that he's not afraid of death but of time.

scenes.heading: INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Murph realizes Professor Brand's equation has been recursive and nonsensical because it assumes time cannot be changed, and she tells him he's been trying to solve it with both arms tied behind his back.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNICATIONS BOOTH, ENDURANCE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brand watches a recorded message from her father on screen discussing the realities of interstellar travel and thinking as a species rather than individuals.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper tells the crew they must choose between Dr. Mann's ice world and Edmunds' desert planet because they lack fuel to visit both; Brand argues for Edmunds' planet based on her field expertise and Gargantua's sterilizing radiation, but Cooper decides they will go to Dr. Mann's planet because he is still transmitting.
scenes.turningPointNote: Critical mission decision made: the crew chooses Dr. Mann's planet over Edmunds', setting the course for the film's climactic betrayal and determining the fate of both Plan A and Plan B.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTER SPACE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Endurance fires thrusters and moves out of orbit around Gargantua.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - LATER
scenes.summary: Brand tells Cooper that if they strike out on Dr. Mann's planet, they'll have to decide whether to return home or push on to Edmunds' planet with Plan B.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUTER SPACE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance passes a golden nebula that dissolves to black clouds.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tom tells Murph they'll lose a third of their crops this season but will make it up by working Nelson's fields next year, and when Murph asks what happened to Nelson, Tom glances at her and heads for the house.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. FARMHOUSE - DUSK
scenes.summary: Murph has dinner with Tom's family and offers to have a friend examine young Coop's lungs after he coughs, telling Lois there are too many memories for her to stay the night.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, NASA - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Murph hurries down a corridor with doctor Getty, who tells her Professor Brand started asking for her after he came to.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NASA - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Professor Brand, dying and hooked to machines, confesses to Murph that he let everyone down and needed her to believe her father was coming back, then reveals he knew Plan A was impossible and begs her forgiveness before dying.
scenes.turningPointNote: Professor Brand's deathbed confession reveals Plan A was always a lie, fundamentally altering Murph's understanding of her father's mission and the fate of humanity on Earth.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMUNICATION ROOM, NASA - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph records a message asking Brand if she knew Plan A was a sham and accusing them of leaving Earth to die.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph's voice plays through the empty Endurance asking if Brand knew Plan A was a sham while Case registers it and the Ranger moves away toward Dr. Mann's planet.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: The Endurance orbits Dr. Mann's planet as the Ranger heads toward it.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Ranger drops through layers of large, mountainous cloud.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper studies a heads-up display of cloud density with concern as they descend.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger scrapes against a cloud formation that turns out to be solid ice, tearing panels from the wing.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper banks away from the ice and glances out at the damage to the wing.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger moves cautiously through the cloudscape like a ship through an ice field.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly and Brand put their helmets on while Tars indicates the beacon's position and Cooper banks the Ranger.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger's gear lowers as it comes to rest at the base of what looks like a large cumulus cloud.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper leads the crew up the ice cloud, crests a ridge, spots a dirty orange dot in the cloudscape, and starts down toward it.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S POD - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The crew arrives at Dr. Mann's weathered and damaged pod half-buried in ice, and Tars starts digging out the hatch.

scenes.heading: INT. DR MANN’S POD - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper opens Dr. Mann's cryo-chamber, rips the seal, and Dr. Mann's eyes flicker open before he grabs Cooper and pulls himself up sobbing, desperately caressing Cooper's face.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME - LATER
scenes.summary: Dr. Mann tells the crew he had set no waking date after his supplies were exhausted, and when Cooper asks about the other astronauts, Dr. Mann learns they are all dead and that this is the last stop before returning home.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Mann leads the crew to the summit to watch the sunset and describes the planet's harsh conditions while they make their way back to shelter.

scenes.heading: INT. DR MANN’S POD - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Dr. Mann tells the crew that the surface has breathable air, organics, and possibly life, and that he has dropped probes over the years but has only mounted limited expeditions due to oxygen constraints.

scenes.heading: INT. DR MANN’S POD - LATER
scenes.summary: Brand watches Murph's message accusing her of knowing Plan A was a sham, Cooper is shocked when Murph asks if he knew and if he left her to die, and Dr. Mann calmly confirms that Professor Brand never hoped to get people off Earth because solving the equation required data from inside a black hole.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper learns the full truth about Plan A's impossibility and confronts the devastating reality that Murph believes he knowingly abandoned her to die, shattering his understanding of the mission.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DUSTY PLAIN - DAWN
scenes.summary: Murph tells Getty that Professor Brand's solution was only half the answer and the other half requires data from inside a black hole, then they drive past refugees and Murph insists they must keep working because Brand gave up but she is still trying to solve it.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANGER, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAWN
scenes.summary: Tars works on the Ranger's wing while Case pilots the lander in to land nearby.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly suggests Cooper make one last attempt at the black hole on his return journey, explaining that a probe crossing the event horizon of the spinning black hole might survive and glimpse the singularity to get quantum data needed to solve the equation.

scenes.heading: EXT. RANGER, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper tells Brand the Ranger is almost ready, and Dr. Mann agrees to let Romilly supervise Tars removing components from Kipp after initially expressing concern about disturbing Kipp's archival functions, then Cooper asks Dr. Mann to show him a landing site.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper follows Dr. Mann down a ridge and into a massive ice canyon, and when Dr. Mann mentions Brand told him why Cooper feels he has to go back, Cooper stops and says he's not leaving until he knows the crew is set up.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph introduces Getty to Lois and Coop, and Getty begins examining Coop while Murph slips upstairs.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph stands in her old bedroom and young Coop tells her his mama lets him play there but he doesn't touch her stuff.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand turns away as the lander touches down in a spray of ice.

scenes.heading: INT. DR MANN’S POD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly watches Tars connect Kipp to his own power and Kipp shows signs of life.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICE CANYON, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Mann tells Cooper that machines can't improvise well because they can't be programmed with a fear of death, and that the survival instinct extending to Cooper's kids is why he's going home.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Getty tells Murph that Tom's family can't stay at the farmhouse, and Tom appears in the doorway asking what this is.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDER, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand is counting flight cases when a wind whips ice at her.

scenes.heading: INT. DR MANN’S POD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly takes his helmet off and peers over Tars' shoulder at Kipp's data screen, confused.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICE FIELD, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Mann rips Cooper's long-range transmitter from his neck and blasts him with his elbow jet, knocking Cooper's helmet off.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dr. Mann's sudden attack on Cooper reveals his betrayal and that he faked all the data, triggering the film's climactic survival crisis and exposing the mission's catastrophic failure.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tom punches Getty and orders Murph to leave, saying he won't abandon the farm where their grandfather and mother are buried, and Murph tells him their father didn't raise him this dumb and that the Earth is poisoning his family.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Mann kicks Cooper over the edge of a crevasse and explains he faked all the data because the planet has nothing, confessing that his survival instinct made him set the beacon to call for rescue even though it doomed the people who came.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph tells Tom their father was never coming back and abandoned them, then leaves the box with young Coop and departs.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICE FIELD, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper pins Dr. Mann and begs him to stop, but Dr. Mann headbutts Cooper's faceplate repeatedly until both helmets crack, then delivers one final blow that shatters Cooper's faceplate and causes ammonia to hiss in.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DUSTY PLAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph drives with Getty, who tells her she did her best, while she stares ahead at the road.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICE FIELD, DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Dr. Mann watches Cooper crawl and tells him the survival instinct drove him and will save humanity, then walks away unable to watch, continuing to talk via radio and asking Cooper if he sees his children yet while listening to him choke.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DUSTY PLAIN - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph and Getty drive past desperate refugees while Dr. Mann's voice recites Dylan Thomas over the radio.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper crawls and chokes while Dr. Mann watches from higher ice, then Cooper grabs the transmitter, pulls off his freezing glove, reconnects it, and screams for Brand to help him.

scenes.heading: EXT. LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand is startled by Cooper's rasping voice over her radio calling for help.

scenes.heading: INT. /EXT. PICKUP TRUCK ON DUSTY PLAIN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Murph spins the truck around and floors it back in the direction they came from.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand jumps into the lander where Case is already firing engines, and she radios Cooper that they're coming.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICE PLAIN, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper gasps for air in the ammonia atmosphere while Brand radios him to breathe as little as possible.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORNFIELDS - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph pulls off the road and cuts through the cornfield.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The lander hurtles recklessly and dangerously fast through the cloudscape, punching through and dodging clouds.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand watches Case fly the lander, praying he's not just guessing.

scenes.heading: INT. DR MANN’S POD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly, perturbed by data that makes no sense, asks Tars to access the archive.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORNFIELDS - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph pulls up in a screech of dust, jumps out, grabs her spare gas can, and throws fuel over the nearby corn stalks.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICE PLAIN, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper flops back onto the ice staring skyward, his eyes watering from poison or memory as he recalls young Murph throwing the watch he gave her.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORNFIELDS - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph sets fire to the corn and jumps in the truck.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The lander sweeps around a towering cumulus and spirals in on the ice plain.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand points and says she sees Cooper.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Tom races out of the house, jumps in Cooper's old truck, and heads out to the fire while calling on the radio.

scenes.heading: EXT. ICE PLAIN, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand leaps from the lander, rips Cooper's helmet off, and thrusts an oxygen mask over his face; Cooper gasps that Mann did this, and Brand realizes the danger and radios Romilly.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper is rescued and reveals Mann's betrayal; Brand realizes Romilly is in danger at Mann's compound

scenes.heading: INT. DR MANN’S POD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Romilly places his hand on the screen to unlock Kipp's archival function, and Kipp explodes.
scenes.turningPointNote: Romilly is killed by the booby-trapped robot, a devastating loss for the crew

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann hears the explosion, sees a black cloud rising, and panic-stricken, switches his radio back on and makes for the Ranger.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand pulls the mask off Cooper, who apologizes to Brand for not following her instincts and tells her Mann lied about the planet; Brand responds with scared realization.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph screeches up to the farmhouse, tells Getty to keep watch, and runs toward the house calling for Lois.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - DAY
scenes.summary: Case spots an explosion on the console at Dr Mann's compound and puts the lander into the air.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann straps into the Ranger and powers it up.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger rises into the air.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann pushes the Ranger into the air.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The lander comes through black smoke and Tars bursts out below, blackened and burned but running toward the lander.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Case swings the lander around and hits the airlock open.

scenes.heading: EXT. DR MANN’S POD - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars leaps up into the airlock as the lander thrusts away.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, THE LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper asks Case for a fix on the Ranger and realizes Mann is pushing into orbit to take control of the Endurance and maroon them.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crew realizes Mann is attempting to steal the Endurance and strand them on the planet

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - DAY
scenes.summary: Lois and Coop head outside with essentials while Murph heads upstairs.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Getty helps Lois and Coop into the truck and nervously glances at the distant fire, calling for Murph to hurry.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger rockets upward through the stratosphere.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper transmits to Dr Mann while Case reveals Tars disabled the autopilot; Cooper learns Mann doesn't know the docking procedure and continues trying to reach him.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann hears Cooper's warning over the radio, ignores him, and switches off the transmitter while looking at the navigation computer.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph looks at the bookshelves, spots the box, and moves over to look inside it.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Getty paces outside while Lois and Coop sit tight in the truck.

scenes.heading: EXT. ORBIT, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger approaches the Endurance in orbit.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann pilots the Ranger alongside the Endurance, attempts auto-docking but is denied authorization, then looks at the manual docking controls.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper peers ahead as they shoot up into orbit and sees the Ranger close to the Endurance; he warns Mann not to attempt docking but receives only static.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann scrambles from the controls to the airlock, focused on his task.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Ranger inches closer to an outer hatch of the Endurance as mechanical grapples try to connect.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann works the docking system, concentrating on the controls.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The grapples pull the Ranger into the Endurance hatch.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The ships clang together with imperfect contact; Dr Mann overrides the hatch lockout and moves to the airlock control.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper asks if Mann is locked on, learns the connection is imperfect, and desperately warns Mann not to open the hatch.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann looks through the hatch window in silence and hits the button to open the outer door.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The outer door of the Ranger slides open as several grapples open and close blindly, trying to seal the join.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper asks what happens if Mann blows the hatch, learns nothing good will result, and orders Case to pull them back while Brand tries to warn Mann.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann is startled by Brand's emergency broadcast warning, justifies his actions over the radio as being for mankind's survival, then pulls the inner lever and is yanked into the airlock by the devastating rush of air.
scenes.turningPointNote: Dr Mann opens the imperfectly sealed hatch, causing explosive decompression that pulls him into the airlock and triggers catastrophic damage to the Endurance

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The escaping air and debris push the Endurance into a slow spin.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr Mann is hammered by debris as the airlock starts to rip apart.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance spins faster and faster as the Ranger is ripped away, fragmenting and shredding the closest module of the ring.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The crew stares in horror as the Endurance spins off its orbit toward the planet; Cooper grabs the controls and hits the thrusters.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Endurance is catastrophically damaged and sent spinning toward the planet, forcing Cooper to attempt a desperate rescue of their only way home

scenes.heading: EXT. ORBIT, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The crippled Endurance spins rapidly toward the stratosphere while the lander pursues it, dodging debris from the destroyed Ranger.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper announces his intention to dock with the spinning Endurance despite Case's objection that there's no point using fuel, and orders Case to analyze the ship's spin.

scenes.heading: EXT. ORBIT, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance plummets toward the atmosphere in a dizzying spin as the lander rockets after it, slowly closing the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper orders Case to prepare to match the Endurance's 67-68 RPM rotation on retro-thrusters, responding to Case's claim that it's impossible by saying it's necessary.

scenes.heading: EXT. ORBIT, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The spinning Endurance begins encountering the stratosphere and starts heating up.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand warns that the Endurance has no heat shield as Cooper checks the lander's speed and pulls back on thrust while approaching from below.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The lander banks sideways, bringing its airlock within feet of the red-hot underside of the spinning Endurance.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper becomes uncertain while watching the spinning hull, but Case tells him this is no time for caution, prompting Cooper to order the retro-thrusters engaged as he warns he might black out.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The lander enters a faster and faster spin as it plummets toward the planet alongside the Endurance.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper and Brand are pulled against their restraints by extreme G-forces as they buffet against the atmosphere, while Tars opens the airlock and sees the Endurance hatch slowly rotating above him.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The glowing hot Endurance and lander plummet spinning toward the ice planet as its curvature rapidly disappears.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRLOCK, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars fires grapples when the hatches line up but they fail to connect as the hatches move out of alignment.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand loses consciousness while Cooper watches the instruments on the verge of red-out, urging Tars to succeed.

scenes.heading: INT. AIRLOCK, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars fires the grapples again when the hatches roll back into alignment and this time they hold successfully.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper successfully docks with the spinning Endurance, preventing its destruction and enabling the mission to continue toward Edmunds' planet.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper registers the successful connection and Case fires retro-rockets to gently slow the spin.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The two joined craft begin to spin more slowly.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper eases back into his seat as the G-force lessens and orders Case to prepare to pull them up as the spin slows to almost nothing.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Parts rip off the Endurance in the heat of the stratosphere.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper eases power into the main thrusters, urging the ship to succeed.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRATOSPHERE, DR MANN’S PLANET - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The lander's thrusters drag both ships back up away from the planet as the heat diminishes.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper pulls back on the controls with relief as Brand regains consciousness, but Case warns they're heading into Gargantua's pull, causing Cooper's smile to fade as he unbuckles.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper and Tars in suits lock down bulkheads amid rushing air and debris while Brand checks the population bomb equipment, and Cooper learns from Tars that they need to hear the damage assessment.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper tells Brand the navigation mainframe is destroyed and they lack life support to reach Earth but might reach Edmunds' planet by using Gargantua's gravity for a powered slingshot, though they don't have enough fuel.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The crippled Endurance falls toward Gargantua's dark center.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper looks ahead at Gargantua, preparing for the maneuver.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The lander detaches and reattaches to the rear of the ring module as Cooper explains they'll use the lander and Ranger 2 as rocket-boosters to escape the black hole's gravity.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper slides into Ranger 2 and checks the systems, noting that the linkages between landers are destroyed.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars runs checks at the lander controls as Cooper explains they'll control manually, and Tars notes he'll be sucked into the black hole when Lander 1 is spent.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand asks why Tars has to detach and Cooper explains they must shed mass to escape the gravity, while Tars notes it's the only way humans have figured out to get somewhere.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper tells Brand that Tars is a robot so he doesn't have to ask him to sacrifice himself.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand angrily calls Cooper an asshole but he pretends the transmission broke up, while Tars confirms it's what they intended.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars explains that if he can transmit the quantum data from inside the black hole, people on Earth might still be saved.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand considers Tars's plan, noting it depends on whether there's still someone on Earth to receive the data.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance accelerates toward Gargantua's darkness.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand tightens her harness as the black hole's gravity makes the ship shudder.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Endurance streaks above the glowing horizon, skirting the blackness beneath.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars looks out at the dark ocean of the black hole.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The ship orbits the black hole with blinding acceleration.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Case announces maximum velocity achieved and orders preparation to fire escape thrusters as the ship shakes with gravitational energy.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars checks his instruments and confirms he's ready.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper checks his instruments and confirms he's ready.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand looks fearfully at the darkness below as Case counts down and hits the button to fire the main engines.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The main engines fire, straining against Gargantua's gravity.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand feels the thrusters strain to lift the craft as Case counts down to fire Lander 1's engines.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tars hits the button to fire Lander 1's engines.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lander 1's engines fire and the Endurance begins rising away from the darkness as Case counts down for Ranger 2's engines.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper hits the button to fire Ranger 2's engines.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ranger 2's engines add thrust, pushing the Endurance higher back into the starlight.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper announces that the maneuver cost them fifty-one years while shaking from the thrust.
scenes.turningPointNote: The slingshot maneuver succeeds but costs the crew fifty-one years due to time dilation, fundamentally altering their relationship to Earth and everyone they left behind.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand jokes that Cooper doesn't sound bad for being a hundred and twenty years old as she holds on tight.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lander 1's engines shut down and Case radios instructions to prepare for detachment on his mark, counting down from three.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand looks over at the lander as Case continues the countdown over radio.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, LANDER 1 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Case completes the countdown to mark and Tars hits the detach switch.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand watches Lander 1 drop away, revealing Cooper in Ranger 2, and she says goodbye to Tars while something in the exchange puzzles her.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lander 1 falls behind as the Endurance continues rising away from Gargantua.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper checks his dwindling fuel supply and compliments Case on his reckless flying over the radio.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ranger 2's engines die out as Lander 1 falls back toward Gargantua.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Case begins the detachment countdown for Ranger 2 and Brand realizes what's happening, unbuckles, flies to the window, and shouts at Cooper asking what he's doing.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper looks at Brand and tells her he has to leave something behind according to Newton's third law as Case continues counting down.

scenes.heading: INT. RING MODULE, ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand presses her helmet against the window and protests that Cooper told her they had enough power.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper reminds Brand they agreed on ninety percent, takes one last look at her crying face with tears caught in her eyelashes, and hits the detach button.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper sacrifices himself by detaching from the Endurance, sending himself into Gargantua to save Brand and the mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ranger 2 drops away from the Endurance into space.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper watches the Endurance accelerate away to a bright point as he falls and his breathing quickens.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARGANTUA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ranger 2 plummets toward Gargantua's blackness as the horizon glows brighter with distorted starlight and plasma jets.

scenes.heading: INT. COCKPIT, RANGER 2 - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper tries to control his breathing, uses retro-rockets to turn the lander downward, gasps at the flaming horizon, and calls for Tars over radio but receives only static.

scenes.heading: INT. ENDURANCE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Brand monitors Cooper's lonely transmissions while crying.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARGANTUA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ranger 2 crosses the event horizon into Gargantua, shuddering with exponentially rising gravitational energy as Cooper screams and plunges into cascading wavelengths of light before cutting to burning corn and men fighting a fire.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper crosses the event horizon of the black hole, entering a realm beyond normal spacetime.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph sits on her childhood bed looking into a box, pulls out the lunar lander model, and looks up at the books as Getty calls for her offscreen.

scenes.heading: EXT. GARGANTUA - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper plunges through alternating spheres of light and dark within the black hole's four-dimensional structure until his fuel cell overloads, he's ejected from Ranger 2, the lander explodes, and he's pulled into a tunnel lined with infinite versions of himself.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper slows down inside the tunnel, desperately reaches out knocking the sides and kicking bricks from the walls to stop himself, discovers the walls are made of tightly packed pages and books as seen from behind a shelf, and pushes a book which moves slightly then harder until it drops.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper discovers he's inside a tesseract constructed from Murph's bookshelf, realizing he can interact with the past.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - MORNING
scenes.summary: Ten-year-old Murph stands startled, staring at the bookshelves where a book has fallen to the floor beside a broken toy.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph looks at the bookshelves, remembering the past event.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper watches ten-year-old Murph cautiously approach and crouch down to pick something up.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph turns the lunar lander model in her hands, thinking.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Ten-year-old Murph stands up holding the broken lunar lander model.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper watches ten-year-old Murph examine the broken lunar lander, shouts her name desperately, sees her turn and leave, then discovers he can orbit her room by rotating through different wall views and punches out books to see his earlier self standing in the doorway with young Murph, then lashes out at the books in frustration.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph feels the desk and puts her hand on the back of the chair, tilting it slightly while remembering.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper watches ten-year-old Murph put a chair on top of the desk as his earlier self nudges the door open.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - EVENING
scenes.summary: Ten-year-old Murph sees the door nudging against the desk and chair and tells her father to just leave now if he's leaving.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper spins around to see his earlier self nudging the door, shouts at himself not to go and not to leave his kids, then punches out books spelling S-T-A-Y while young Murph watches fascinated, but his earlier self lifts the chair and enters, ignoring the message.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph picks up her notebook, opens it to find the word STAY, and looks up at the books with realization.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TESSERACT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper cries with frustration, begs Murph not to let him leave, and smashes a book from the shelf as his earlier self turns back briefly then leaves, after which Cooper rests his head against the books sobbing.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph lowers her notebook, moves to the bookshelves in awe, and realizes her father was her ghost all along and that he's going to save them, crying with joy.
scenes.turningPointNote: Murph realizes her father has been communicating with her from inside the black hole and is trying to save humanity.

scenes.heading: INT. THE TESSERACT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper sobs until Tars contacts him over radio, revealing they both survived and that beings constructed this three-dimensional space within five dimensions to allow Cooper to understand it, and Cooper realizes gravity crosses dimensions including time and that he can use it to send a message, then pulls himself along the world lines of the tesseract.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper understands the tesseract's purpose and realizes he can use gravity to transmit data across time to save humanity.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph stands looking at her old notebook page that says STAY as Getty calls for her urgently offscreen.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE TESSERACT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper watches ten-year-old Murph looking out the window and argues with Tars that they brought themselves here to communicate with the three-dimensional world, then pushes off looking through gaps in the books at Murph's dust-filled bedroom and asks Tars to feed him NASA's coordinates in binary.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph runs her finger along the dusty windowsill, turns to look around the room frustrated, and asks if there's something else there.

scenes.heading: INT. TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper realizes he brought himself here and that they are the bridge to communicate with the three-dimensional world, then moves to another iteration of Murph's room where ten-year-old Murph jumps up, grabs the watch, and runs out.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph looks at the watch remembering, sees the second hand twitch, and drops it back into the box.

scenes.heading: INT. TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper pushes himself along the world line of books, realizes they chose Murph not him to save the world, and watches ten-year-old Murph return to her bedroom sobbing and place the watch on the shelf.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph puts the box back on the shelf and sighs.

scenes.heading: INT. TESSERACT - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper races faster down the world lines, explaining to Tars that the beings have infinite time and space but no way to find what they need, but he can find Murph through love and tell her how to find the moment, then looks at the watch on the shelf and realizes he can tell her how to save the world.

scenes.heading: INT. MURPH’S BEDROOM - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Forty-year-old Murph turns to leave but pauses when Getty calls urgently, goes back to the box, and pulls out the watch, staring at it and wondering.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - TWILIGHT
scenes.summary: Getty holds a tire iron watching Tom's truck approach while Murph bursts out of the house and tells Tom their father came back and is going to save them, holding up the watch with its second hand flicking back and forth.

scenes.heading: INT. PROFESSOR BRAND’S OFFICE - DAY
scenes.summary: Murph furiously transcribes the movements of the watch's second hand in Professor Brand's office.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, NASA - LATER
scenes.summary: Murph runs down the corridor with papers in hand and bumps into Getty without stopping.

scenes.heading: INT. LAUNCH FACILITY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Murph runs to the catwalk railing above the enormous construction, shouts Eureka to the thousands of workers below, grins at Getty, and throws her papers over the railing while shouting Eureka again.
scenes.turningPointNote: Murph solves the gravity equation using the data Cooper transmitted through the watch, achieving the breakthrough needed to save humanity.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE TESSERACT - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper asks Tars if it worked and Tars thinks it might have because the bulk beings are closing the tesseract, then Cooper realizes the beings aren't external entities but future humans trying to help, and as the tesseract rapidly collapses back into five dimensions Cooper braces himself and is swept away.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper completes his mission and the tesseract collapses, ejecting him back into normal space.

scenes.heading: INT. OUTER SPACE - LATER
scenes.summary: Cooper floats dead or unconscious near Saturn as two Rangers approach in the distance.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper wakes up in a hospital room with a nurse and doctor watching him, and the doctor tells him he's one hundred twenty-four years old and was found with only minutes of oxygen left.

scenes.heading: INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - DAY
scenes.summary: An Administrator leads Cooper through a hangar showing him sleek next-generation Rangers and mentions doing a school paper on Cooper's life on Earth.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOWN SQUARE, COOPER STATION - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Administrator walks Cooper through an ordinary-looking town square and mentions making a suggestion to Ms. Cooper that she thought was perfect.

scenes.heading: EXT. FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Administrator leads Cooper through a cornfield to the preserved old farmhouse where a monitor plays old-timer footage that changes to a familiar old lady as Cooper passes without noticing.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper enters the farmhouse kitchen museum exhibit and recognizes the articulated machine found near Saturn, asking the Administrator to get him a new power source for it.

scenes.heading: INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - EVENING
scenes.summary: Cooper watches from a catwalk as the last Ranger returns from patrol and is wheeled into place among the line of ships.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, FARMHOUSE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cooper adjusts TARS' settings on the kitchen table, setting honesty to ninety-five percent and humor to sixty percent.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL WAITING ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: Cooper arrives at a hospital waiting room and a nurse tells him the family is all inside with someone who has been in cryo-sleep for almost two years.

scenes.heading: INT. HOSPITAL ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Cooper enters a hospital room surrounded by Murph's family, reunites with his elderly daughter Murph who confirms she knew he was her ghost and shows him the watch, while her family leaves them alone.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper and Murph reunite after decades apart, confirming the resolution of their relationship and the success of his mission to save humanity.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Brand stands crying in her suit and helmet on Edmunds' desert planet, watching Case excavate a pod buried under a rock fall.

scenes.heading: EXT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: After a mechanic leaves the hangar, TARS unfolds from the shadows and unlocks a door while Cooper darts inside.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Brand kneels before a small cross with Edmunds' nameplate, unseals her helmet, and pulls it off.

scenes.heading: INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cooper and TARS scurry down the line of ships as TARS works a hatch mechanism while Cooper keeps watch.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: Brand breathes the air of Edmunds' planet with her helmet off.

scenes.heading: INT. RANGER IN HANGAR, COOPER STATION - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Cooper straps into the pilot's chair of a Ranger with TARS beside him as the outer doors slide open to reveal the blackness of space.
scenes.turningPointNote: Cooper chooses to leave Cooper Station and launch into space, rejecting the settled life offered to him and setting out on a new journey.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DUSK
scenes.summary: Brand looks at the setting sun on Edmunds' planet.

scenes.heading: INT. HANGAR, COOPER STATION - MORNING
scenes.summary: The Mechanic opens the hangar in the morning and discovers one of the ships is missing from the row.

scenes.heading: EXT. EDMUNDS’ DESERT PLANET - DUSK
scenes.summary: Brand turns from the dwindling light and heads down through the twilight toward camp as the film fades out.
